summary.narrative: Governor McMaster has maintained a consistent record on fiscal conservatism and opposing new taxes, while advancing significant infrastructure investments. His administration has successfully resisted organized labor actions and maintained pro-business policies, though some education reform promises remain incomplete.

promises.text: Oppose any new taxes on South Carolina residents
promises.evidence: McMaster has consistently blocked new tax proposals, stating the state has 'plenty' without putting 'yet another tax on the people of South Carolina.'

promises.text: Make major investments in state infrastructure
promises.evidence: In 2025, McMaster proposed allocating $1.1 billion to state construction projects, one of the largest single-year infrastructure investments in state history.

promises.text: Resist organized labor actions threatening South Carolina ports
promises.evidence: McMaster actively opposed the International Longshoremen's Association boycott targeting the Port of Charleston, with the issue escalating to the U.S. Supreme Court.

promises.text: Maintain South Carolina as a right-to-work state
promises.evidence: South Carolina has maintained its right-to-work status throughout McMaster's tenure, with the governor vocally defending these protections.

promises.text: Increase teacher pay to the national average
promises.evidence: Teacher salaries have increased under McMaster's budgets but South Carolina still falls short of the national average for teacher compensation.

promises.text: Expand school choice options for South Carolina families
promises.evidence: McMaster has supported education savings account legislation and charter school expansion, with incremental progress made through the legislature.

promises.text: Fully fund rural broadband expansion across the state
promises.evidence: While initial funding was allocated, full deployment to underserved rural areas has faced delays and implementation challenges.
Despite campaign rhetoric about reducing tax burdens, South Carolina still maintains a state income tax with no legislation passed to eliminate it.
